.ddx/executions/20260504T021759-c712d0e8/prompt.md:5:The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

.ddx/executions/20260504T022245-36a2ca7b/prompt.md:742:+          "body": "Architectural conflict with open bead ddx-eccc6efb (ADR-022 step 7), plus the bead materially exceeds the size-check thresholds. Human triage required to resolve the design conflict before any unification work commits. ## Summary The bead asks to UNIFY the parallel ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec / cobra-flag / HTTP-request structs into one canonical `ExecuteLoopSpec` struct used at every layer. But the open ADR-022 step 7 epic (ddx-eccc6efb, status=open) takes the opposite direction: DELETE `ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec` entirely and replace the in-process server-spawn path with `exec ddx work` (subprocess), at which point there are no parallel structs because the server stops marshalling a spec at all — it just builds an argv vector. These are mutually-exclusive architectural directions for the same code. Landing this bead's unification would either: (a) be partially undone when ADR-022 step 7 deletes the unified struct, or (b) lock in the unified-struct design and require ADR-022 step 7 to be re-scoped or abandoned. The bead's \"SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS\" section names dc157075, 9d55601f, and a946c744, but is silent on ddx-eccc6efb / ADR-022 step 7. .ddx/executions/20260504T144958-1da26656/prompt.md:3:    <title>ADR-023 + FEAT-004/010/011 amendments: bead-lifecycle quality enforcement policy</title>
.ddx/executions/20260504T144958-1da26656/prompt.md:5:PROBLEM
Per epic ddx-a9d130d0: bead-lifecycle quality framework needs an ADR documenting the policy + amendments to existing FEATs to formally adopt the lint/triage hooks and the sub-skill packaging change.

ROOT CAUSE
Today no policy artifact exists for bead-quality enforcement. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead ddx-06b77652 documents P7 as a principle but does not specify the enforcement mechanism, waiver model, staged rollout, or recovery UX. Existing FEAT docs are stale on relevant points:
- FEAT-011 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-011-skills.md:180 says DDx ships exactly one skill, but .agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md already demonstrates nested sub-skill packaging.
- FEAT-004 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-004-beads.md:150 owns bead validation hooks but doesn't describe authoring-quality lint or post-attempt triage.
- FEAT-010 at docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-executions.md:21 documents the layer-1/2/3 invocation model but doesn't spec hook insertion points for ddx try / ddx work.

PROPOSED FIX
1. Author ADR-023 at docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-bead-lifecycle-quality-policy.md covering:
   - Policy: lint pre-dispatch + triage post-attempt; both invoke the same bead-lifecycle sub-skill
   - Staged rollout: WARN-ONLY default → BLOCK opt-in after baseline confirms
   - Waiver model: rubric-level by bead-type (doc/epic/deletion skip certain criteria, per docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md:219); per-bead lint-waiver:<criterion> label for rare exceptions; --force --reason records an event (does not silently bypass)
   - Fail-open hook behavior (P1 from RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES): infrastructure failure proceeds; only valid low lint score blocks (in BLOCK mode)
   - Operator recovery UX: blocked dispatch prints missing fields + suggested ddx bead update commands

2. Amend FEAT-004 (beads): add §"Authoring quality lint" describing the rubric, sub-skill location, waiver storage (label-based), and tracker metadata (no new schema fields; lint output ephemeral in evidence dir).

3. Amend FEAT-010 (executions): add §"Quality hooks" describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook insertion points in ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime; OutcomeReason field beside existing Disrupted in ExecuteBeadReport.

4. Amend FEAT-011 (skills): replace "ships exactly one skill" with the nested workflow-skill model demonstrated by bead-breakdown/, replay-bead/, etc. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths per codex review (skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/).

5. Cross-link: ADR-023 references RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652) + evidence (ddx-f339c399) + bead-authoring-template.md as the canonical template.

NON-SCOPE
- New FEAT (codex: not warranted; existing FEATs cover the surface)
- ADR for the in_progress-eligibility bug (separate concern)
- Cross-project skill packaging (deferred per FEAT-015)
- Schema changes to bead JSON (waivers via labels; no new fields)

Status: Proposed (Accepted after operator review).

2. FEAT-004-beads.md gains §"Authoring quality lint" (~50 lines) describing the lint hook + waiver storage. Cross-link to ADR-023.

3. FEAT-010-executions.md gains §"Quality hooks" (~50 lines) describing PreDispatchLintHook + PostAttemptTriageHook + OutcomeReason field. Cross-link to ADR-023.

4. FEAT-011-skills.md updated: stale "ships exactly one skill" replaced with nested workflow-skill model. Document the 5 shipped-skill paths. Cross-link to ADR-023.

5. ADR-023 cross-links: RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES (ddx-06b77652), evidence (ddx-f339c399), bead-authoring-template.md, audits-1+2.

6. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>]. Stage docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-023-*.md + docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-{004,010,011}-*.md only.

7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

8. Manual verification: 'grep -l ADR-023 docs/helix/' shows the ADR + 3 FEAT amendments.

.ddx/executions/20260503T225413-91120fa0/prompt.md:3:    <title>ADR-022 step 7: server-spawn path migration (workers.go → exec ddx work)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260503T230228-6c8b4237/prompt.md:3:    <title>ADR-022 step 7: server-spawn path migration (workers.go → exec ddx work)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260503T230228-6c8b4237/prompt.md:45:+    "title": "ADR-022 step 7: server-spawn path migration (workers.go → exec ddx work)",
.ddx/executions/20260503T230228-6c8b4237/prompt.md:607:+          "body": "Bead scope significantly exceeds the stated \"~200 LOC + test updates\" estimate; safe execution requires decomposition into 3 child beads, but child-bead creation must originate from the main repo (not this execution worktree) to get clean ddx-prefixed IDs. ## Why this attempt declined to commit The bead asks for an end-to-end migration of the server-spawn path from in-process execution to subprocess `exec ddx work`. Six tightly-coupled ACs touch the most intricate file in the server package. ### Actual surface (vs stated estimate) cli/internal/server/workers.go is 1,959 lines. The current `runWorker` (lines 612–926, ~315 lines) is a dense in-process orchestrator that knits together: - in-process `agent.ExecuteBeadWorker` construction - per-project `LandCoordinator` single-flight serialization - in-process post-merge `DefaultBeadReviewer` - in-process `escalation.CostCapTracker` (per-worker billed cost accumulation) - in-process `RoutePreflight` (typed-incompatibility gate via FizeauService) - per-attempt `singleTierAttempt` closure with config resolution + gate evaluation + leftover-evidence safety net - progress channel piped to SSE subscribers via `drainProgress` - watchdog phase-stall tracking via shared workerHandle.lastPhaseTS - terminal-state preservation (stopped/reaped) merged with loop result - BeadWorkerFactory injection point for test fakes Replacing the in-process call with `exec ddx work` collapses all of those in-process couplings — they now run inside the child process — but the parent server still needs equivalent observability/control via: - /api/workers/{id}/event ingestion (already exists from step 1) for progress - PID + signal escalation for watchdog/Stop (PID field exists; signal plumbing is partial) - worker-events.jsonl tail or HTTP-mirrored events for SSE bridging - Cross-process Stop / wakeCh equivalent ### Test surface cli/internal/server/workers_test.go alone is 1,107 lines, plus 9 sibling workers_*_test.go files and graphql_subscription_worker_test.go that all assert in-process semantics. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either: - a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or - deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface). This is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\" ### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end) \"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\" Even with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). That is a substantial new integration test — comparable to the workers_e2e_test.go suite — distinct from the refactor proper. ### Size-check verdict - More than ~6 ACs across unrelated subsystems? 6 ACs, but several (in-process-replacement refactor, struct deletion, full test sweep, real-harness integration) are independently sized engineering deliverables. - AC mixes implementation, integration tests, and end-to-end scenarios? Yes (AC #1–4 = refactor, AC #5 = new integration test, AC #6 = full lefthook gate). - More than ~500 LOC across more than ~5 files? Yes — workers.go rewrite (~400), server.go handler (~70), workers_test.go sweep (~600+), sibling test sweep (~500+), new exec/stub plumbing (~200), new e2e integration test (~300). Estimated 1,500–2,500 LOC of changes. Many tests inject `BeadWorkerFactory` for control over the in-process worker; with subprocess execution, those tests need to be replaced by either:\n\n- a built-in subprocess-stub binary (e.g. `go test -test.run … -ddx-fake-work-stub` reentry pattern) the parent execs in tests, or\n- deletion as no-longer-applicable (semantics now belong to ddx work's own test surface).\n\nThis is the bulk of the work, not \"test updates.\"\n\n### AC #5 (real-harness end-to-end)\n\n\"server-spawned worker (via the new exec path) registers via the new endpoints AND successfully invokes the claude harness (real claude binary, not a stub) on a fixture bead AND completes the attempt with a commit\"\n\nEven with the fall-back to a scripted-claude-stub, this requires: a clean fixture repo with a real ready bead, a stub or real claude binary on $PATH inside the test harness, a server bound to a free port, the new exec path wired in, the worker registering against the running server's /api/workers/register, and an end-to-end commit landing through the LandCoordinator (which has moved out of the parent process). ABSORBS

EXPECTED WORKFLOW
1. Run ddx ac run --check production-reachability against the current worktree. Capture violation list.
2. For each violation, decide: WIRE (add to production code path) or DELETE (function is genuinely obsolete).
3. File one child bead per violation cluster (group by package or by originating story for traceability).
4. Drain the children. Each closes either via wiring PR or deletion PR.
5. Final pass: ddx ac run returns status=pass with no violations and no // wiring:pending annotations remaining (or all annotations cite still-open future beads).

DECISION RULE per violation
- If the symbol's originating bead's AC describes desired runtime behavior → WIRE.
- If the symbol was added speculatively or the design has moved on → DELETE; reopen the originating bead with obsolescence rationale, or close as obsolete.
- If neither is clear within 15 min of investigation → annotate // wiring:pending <follow-up-bead> and file the follow-up.

2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

.ddx/executions/20260503T195715-4725673a/prompt.md:5:Per principle P7 (RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead): a bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute without hand-curation. Today many beads punt the investigation, omit concrete test names, and leave non-scope implicit. Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been compensating; this bead surfaces the gap and produces a template.

ROOT CAUSE
Beads were authored expecting the executor to investigate first. Sub-agents (and ddx try / ddx work workers) need investigation done UPFRONT to execute reliably. Without it, the executor either:
- Gives up (no_changes after limited investigation), OR
- Produces wrong work (wrong file, wrong scope), OR
- Asks for clarification (not possible in autonomous drain)

ROOT CAUSE 2
No formal authoring template exists. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md describe bead types but not bead structure or quality bar. New beads inherit whatever the operator was thinking at the moment.

SCOPE
1. Audit 20 representative open beads (mix of priorities, areas, types) against a quality rubric.
2. Identify common gaps; quantify (how many beads miss test names, how many lack file:line cites, etc.).
3. Produce bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/.
4. Update CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and DDx skill at .agents/skills/ddx/ to reference the template.
5. Identify which 10-15 high-impact open beads warrant retrofit (the next round of execution work depends on them).

QUALITY RUBRIC (the audit measures each bead against)
- (a) Title: one-line scope clarity
- (b) Description: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope
- (c) AC #1-N: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names
- (d) AC includes 'wired-in' assertion when introducing new code paths
- (e) AC includes go test command + lefthook gate
- (f) Labels: phase + area + kind + cross-refs (adr/spec/prevention)
- (g) Parent + Deps: explicit
- (h) Description reads as sufficient sub-agent prompt (no operator hand-curation needed)

Score per bead: 8/8 = passes; less = needs retrofit.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Retrofitting beads (separate follow-up beads filed from the audit's recommendations)
- Auto-validating bead structure on creation (future tooling bead)
- Reorganizing existing bead labels or hierarchies

INTERSECTIONS
- RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES bead (this audit operationalizes P7)
- Retrofit recommendations from this audit gate the dispatch of refactor children C4-C13 (bead retrofit MUST land before sub-agent dispatch with verbatim prompts)

2. The audited 20 beads are listed by ID with per-criterion pass/fail and a 'needs retrofit' verdict.
3. Aggregate stats: % of beads passing each criterion; top 3 most-common gaps.
4. docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md exists with: template fields (title/description/AC structure), examples (one good bead, one weak bead with annotations), authoring checklist.
5. CLAUDE.md gains a 'When filing beads' section pointing at the template.
6. .agents/skills/ddx/ skill (and the embedded copies under .claude/skills/ddx/) updated to require template fields when creating beads.

8. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].
9. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

NO SUB-AGENT DISPATCH from this work — the audit is read-only research. The retrofit beads are filed by operator after reviewing the audit recommendations.

.ddx/executions/20260503T111826-4bacc8be/prompt.md:5:Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.

.ddx/executions/20260503T110552-7129c1c3/prompt.md:5:The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

.ddx/executions/20260503T021424-dac100b6/prompt.md:5:Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

Proposed architectural shift: WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API.

DESIGN PROPOSALS (this TD chooses among them)

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

INTEGRATION WITH IN-FLIGHT WORK

This TD intersects:
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): the spec becomes the registration payload — single struct still applies
- ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor epic: the work package boundary aligns with worker autonomy; C9 (StopCondition) becomes worker-side state
- ddx-4c51d33e (cross-project leak): workers identify their project at registration; server enforces project boundaries via session token
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): becomes natural — worker stays alive, polls for work, doesn't exit on empty queue

IMPLEMENTATION OUTLINE (post-TD)
1. Define API contract + handlers (cli/internal/server/worker_api.go)
2. Add worker client (cli/internal/agent/work/client.go) implementing the API
3. Add in-process API implementation for --local mode (cli/internal/agent/work/local_api.go)
4. Migrate execute_bead_loop / drain_loop to consume the API instead of direct store access
5. Migrate server worker spawn to register-then-poll instead of inline-execution

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS TD
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or new FEAT) describing the worker contract
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups

DEPS
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads

2. ADR explicitly evaluates Proposals A, B, C (or articulates a fourth) and documents the chosen design with rationale.
3. ADR includes the worker-server API contract: full request/response schemas for register, heartbeat, next-bead, event, result, disconnect.
4. ADR includes sequence diagrams (text/mermaid) for: worker registration, bead claim, execute+report, server-restart recovery, graceful shutdown.

.ddx/executions/20260503T025022-29ef49b0/prompt.md:5:Multiple test/demo workflows need a clean ddx-initialized git repo without polluting this main project. Today each test creates its own t.TempDir() fixture; integration tests do ad-hoc setup; acceptance demos have nowhere to go.

USE CASES (current + planned)
- ADR-022 acceptance demo (Stage 4 of design-phase plan): run 2 workers, restart server mid-execution, verify worker resilience without polluting the ddx repo
- Integration tests that need a real .ddx/ directory + bead store + workers/ + executions/
- bd/br interchange tests
- Cross-project leak tests (need >1 project — already partially used in ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1 tests)
- Manual operator walkthroughs / demos for new features
- "What does ddx look like in a fresh project?" docs

DESIGN OPTIONS

Option 1: A SCRIPT that builds a fixture repo on demand
- scripts/build-fixture-repo.sh <dest> [--profile minimal|standard|multi-project|federated]
- Creates a tmp git repo, runs ddx init, optionally adds N seed beads, optionally registers projects
- Tests call it via testing.T helper; demos call it from the operator's shell
- Cleanup is operator/test responsibility (rm -rf <dest>)

Option 2: A FIXTURE-REPO BINARY (ddx fixture create)
- ddx fixture create <dest> --profile standard
- Same outputs as Option 1 but discoverable via --help
- Could be a hidden subcommand under ddx (Hidden: true) since it's dev infrastructure
- Uniform with rest of CLI

Option 3: GO TESTING HELPER (testing-only, not exposed to operator)
- testutil.NewFixtureRepo(t, profile) → projectPath
- Auto-cleanup via t.Cleanup()
- Doesn't help acceptance demos but cleanest for unit/integration tests

RECOMMEND: Hybrid. Option 1 (script) for demo + manual use; Option 3 (Go helper) wraps the script for tests so cleanup is automatic. Option 2 deferred unless operators ask for it.

PROFILES TO SUPPORT (initial)
- minimal: empty .ddx/, no beads
- standard: .ddx/ + 5 mixed-priority sample beads + sample personas + sample skills
- multi-project: 2 registered projects, each with their own beads (for cross-project tests)
- federated: hub + spoke setup (for ADR-007 federation tests)

NOT IN SCOPE
- Mocking the agent service (separate concern; tests use stub fizeau already)
- Pre-populating with real LLM execution traces (deterministic test runs use fixtures, not live LLMs)
- Auto-cleanup beyond t.Cleanup() (operators run rm -rf)

DEPS
- Independent; can land anytime.

.ddx/executions/20260502T230833-36ef6add/prompt.md:5:The current .ddx/beads.jsonl backend (atomic JSONL writes wrapped in flock; events accumulating per-bead; archive split per ADR-004) is showing strain:

1. Multi-worker contention via the git tracker-commit step (see ddx-da11a34a — the file lock around git add/commit is a v1 patch, not the real fix)
2. Events array per bead is unbounded and dominates archive size
3. Cross-machine concurrency is not addressed (Story 14 federation will require it)
4. The current model bakes one process-local store + git-as-replication; doesn't compose cleanly with a real database

Two backend paths under consideration:

**Axon (PRIMARY, per user inclination)**: integrate as the first DDx-side consumer of axon. User owns the stack; tight integration possible; proves axon's value as a substrate. Requires authoring an axon backend implementing the bead schema's storage contract (per ADR-004 / SD-004 — collection abstraction, claim semantics, atomic writes, JSONL interchange compatibility for bd/br).

**bd / doltdb (FALLBACK)**: delegate to bd which already speaks doltdb (git-for-data). Trivial integration; downside is tracking an external codebase as a hard dependency.

This epic frames the evaluation, designs the integration, prototypes, and migrates. Children break down the work. The lock-contention patch (ddx-da11a34a) remains relevant as stability scaffolding while this plays out — when axon ships and is adopted, the JSONL+git path may retire entirely (or stay as a portable interchange backend per FEAT-004's bd/br semantics).
.ddx/executions/20260502T231014-7358ae48/prompt.md:5:Author a Technical Design (TD) document for replacing the JSONL+flock+git bead backend with an axon-backed implementation. Cover: (a) data model mapping — how Bead structs and the events log map onto axon collections/streams; (b) claim/lock semantics — how 'claim a ready bead' becomes an axon op, including multi-worker fairness and cross-machine concurrency (Story 14 federation); (c) archive policy — how closed-bead externalization (.ddx/attachments + .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl per ADR-004) carries over; (d) JSONL interchange — keep bd/br import/export compatibility per FEAT-004; (e) failure modes and recovery; (f) migration story (forward refs to migration-tool child). Cite ADR-004/SD-004 and ddx-da11a34a (current lock-contention patch). Output the TD under docs/ at an appropriate path. No code changes.

.ddx/executions/20260502T205525-d2423a87/prompt.md:5:Author docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md.

Source material (consume in order):
1. /tmp/ddx-axon-backend-plan.md — full integration plan with Axon's external interface mapped + GraphQL pivot + locked decisions
2. Cross-repo tracking bead axon-82b6f7b2 — full DDx-on-axon adoption checklist (links to all required axon-side capabilities)

LOCKED DECISIONS (do not relitigate; bake into TD):
1. Deployment: separate axon-server, operator-managed. DDx does NOT spawn or supervise Axon.
2. Auth: localhost-only by default; ts-net for remote. Axon already supports ts-net per its serve.rs (Tailscale mode + LocalAPI whois + role tags). Reuses ADR-006.
3. Events storage: separate ddx_bead_events collection with event_of links (Option B). Two collections in Axon.
4. Offline posture: refuse all bead ops when Axon unreachable. No local cache, no WAL.
5. Wire transport: GraphQL only via genqlient-generated Go client. NOT gRPC. Subscriptions over WebSocket for live updates.

Cross-repo dependencies (tracked in axon-82b6f7b2):
- axon-05c1019d (P2): GraphQL pattern-query support — needed for single-query ddx bead ready/blocked. Day 1 ships with two-phase fallback.
- FEAT-013 secondary indexes (P1 Draft in axon): needed for status-field query performance at >1000 beads.
- FEAT-017 schema evolution (P1 Draft in axon): needed for clean DDx schema upgrades over time. Day 1 ships with DDx-side lazy migration on read.
- ts-net auth: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon. No coordination needed.
- GraphQL CRUD + subscriptions: ALREADY SHIPPED in axon.
- Multi-namespace/database: ALREADY SHIPPED.

Adoption gates (per axon-82b6f7b2):
- Alpha: requires axon-05c1019d + everything already-shipped. Acceptable to ship with table-scan perf.
- Beta: requires FEAT-013 (indexes) for >1000-bead performance.
- GA: requires FEAT-013 + FEAT-017 + axon-05c1019d all landed.

Defaults the TD can adopt without rechecking with operator (revisit only if implementation surfaces a problem):
- Schema versioning: DDx-side lazy migrate on read until FEAT-017 lands; switch to axon-native then.
- ddx bead ready Day 1: two-phase GraphQL query.
- Backup/DR: operator-managed via Axon's own backing-store DR.

Output: TD-<NNN>-axon-bead-backend.md with sections for: GraphQL schema mapping (DDx bead → Axon entity); operation mapping (DDx CLI → GraphQL queries/mutations/subscriptions); deployment + config; concurrency model (OCC via expectedVersion); migration plan; test plan; cross-repo coordination notes (links to axon-82b6f7b2 + FEAT-013/017).

.ddx/executions/20260502T192946-f3e84c05/prompt.md:5:Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.

.ddx/executions/20260502T193411-c46ce0f7/prompt.md:5:Sibling of ddx-673833f4. After the TD + reconciliation bead land, this bead adds 4 CI guard tests that prevent future state-machine drift.

Each test goes in cli/internal/bead/ or a dedicated cli/internal/bead/sync_test.go file:

1. **Schema enum ↔ Go status constants synchronized**: parse cli/internal/bead/schema/bead-record.schema.json status enum; assert matches the exported canonical Status list in cli/internal/bead/types.go. CI fails on mismatch.

3. **TD doc ↔ schema enum synchronized**: parse docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-NNN-bead-state-machine.md persisted-status section; assert it lists exactly the schema enum. Forces TD amendment when schema changes.

4. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip**: take a fresh bd-export fixture (DDx-side fixture file under cli/internal/bead/testdata/ if not already present); export → import via DDx; assert the round-trip preserves all fields including new labels/events. ADR-004 compatibility regression guard.

Each test must be deterministic, fast (<1s each), and have a clear failure message that points the developer at the exact mismatch location.

.ddx/executions/20260502T193536-5a586700/prompt.md:5:ADR-004 (Accepted) decided to generalize .ddx/beads.jsonl into a named-collection abstraction with separate archive and attachment storage, but the implementation hasn't shipped. Today the single beads.jsonl carries the entire history (5.4MB and growing) and the events array on closed beads is the dominant size driver. This epic implements the architecture per ADR-004.

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold (e.g. >4MB), age-based (closed >30d), or count-based (>500 closed). ADR-004 doesn't pick one.
- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

References: ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004, recent observation that beads.jsonl crossed the 5MB lefthook threshold during the 2026-05 redesign drain.

- Does Axon's ts-net story align with ADR-006 (Tailscale tsnet listener) or require something different?

.ddx/executions/20260502T114559-db0755dd/prompt.md:5:ADR-004 (Accepted) decided to generalize .ddx/beads.jsonl into a named-collection abstraction with separate archive and attachment storage, but the implementation hasn't shipped. Today the single beads.jsonl carries the entire history (5.4MB and growing) and the events array on closed beads is the dominant size driver. This epic implements the architecture per ADR-004.

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold (e.g. >4MB), age-based (closed >30d), or count-based (>500 closed). ADR-004 doesn't pick one.
- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

References: ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004, recent observation that beads.jsonl crossed the 5MB lefthook threshold during the 2026-05 redesign drain.

.ddx/executions/20260502T204932-0e555908/prompt.md:5:Add a language-agnostic check protocol to ddx so projects can declare mechanical pre-merge gates that ddx try evaluates before any worktree is merged back. Solves the recurring "implemented but not wired" failure mode at the platform layer rather than per-project.

CONTEXT
- Audit at /tmp/agent-surface-dead-code-audit.md surfaced 4 functions with tests but no production caller; reviewer didn't catch it because the diff "looked right" and tests passed.
- ac_structural_check.go (cli/internal/agent/) is the existing seed: parses claims from AC prose for test_name/file_deleted/field_removed. It can't catch the unwired case because a unit test naming the function satisfies the claim vacuously.
- Prior plan considered embedding Go-specific call-graph analysis directly in cli/internal/agent/. Rejected as a boundary violation (CLAUDE.md: "DDx provides primitives; HELIX and others provide opinions"). Each language ecosystem has its own dead-code tooling (Go: deadcode/callgraph; Python: vulture; TS: ts-prune/knip; Rust: cargo-udeps).

DESIGN — protocol (declarative, plugin-based)
- Discovery: .ddx/checks/*.yaml each declares one check: { name, command, when: pre_merge, applies_to: { paths: [globs] | always, labels: [...] } }
- Inputs to check command (env vars): BEAD_ID, DIFF_BASE, DIFF_HEAD, PROJECT_ROOT, EVIDENCE_DIR, RUN_ID
- Output contract: check writes ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with schema { status: pass|block|error, message: string, violations: [{file, line, symbol, kind, detail}] }
- Exit code 0 = ran-to-completion (status field is authoritative); non-zero = check itself errored (treated as BLOCK with status=error)
- Hook point: pre_merge — runs in the parent (after worker's worktree work is done, BEFORE the merge-back into integration branch). BLOCK aborts the merge; the worktree stays preserved per existing conventions for human resolution.
- Bypass: bead-level annotation checks_bypass: [{name: <check>, reason: <text>, bead: <bead-id>}] recorded in events. No CLI flag — bypass must be tracked.
- Standalone: ddx ac run <bead-id> [--check <name>] runs the same checks locally so devs can iterate without spawning a full try attempt.

WHY pre_merge (NOT pre_close in the worker)
- User directive: "We can't afford to merge this kind of dead code." Worker-side checks could be bypassed by a worker that ignores BLOCK; parent-side gate cannot.
- Worker still gets feedback via ddx ac run for local iteration.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Multiple lifecycle hook points beyond pre_merge (add later if needed).
- Check dependencies / ordering (independent + parallel until proven insufficient).
- Plugin manifests, capability flags, version negotiation.
- Auto-discovery beyond .ddx/checks/*.yaml.
- Retry / timeout policy (project owns; checks should be fast).
- Any specific language check (separate beads).

OUT OF SCOPE: REACH-GO (the Go-specific deadcode-based check) and REACH-BACKFILL (the systematic application across cli/) are separate beads that depend on this one.

FILES (anticipated)
- cli/internal/checks/protocol.go — config schema + JSON I/O types
- cli/internal/checks/loader.go — read .ddx/checks/*.yaml
- cli/internal/checks/runner.go — subprocess invocation, env injection, output parsing, evidence dir mgmt
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go — wire pre_merge call before merge step; BLOCK aborts merge
- cli/cmd/ac.go — ddx ac run subcommand
- cli/internal/checks/*_test.go — table-driven tests for protocol contract
.ddx/executions/20260502T205402-810674cb/prompt.md:5:Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.

.ddx/executions/20260502T034518-54992614/prompt.md:5:ddx doc audit reports 87 duplicate_id and 2 missing_dep issues, all pre-existing (not caused by the 2026-05 redesign). Two distinct sources observed during the redesign verification:

1. **Migration leftovers**: docs/resources/ still contains agent-harness-ac.md, agent-harness-ac-work.md, ai-agent-frameworks-2025.md, microsoft-ai-agents-2025.md, model-catalog.md, spec-driven.md AND README.md, despite B2 (ddx-a812e176) having moved them under docs/helix/00-discover/. The duplicate IDs (e.g. AC-AGENT-001 in both docs/resources/ and docs/helix/00-discover/research/) are because B2 created the new artifacts but didn't delete the originals. Fix: delete docs/resources/*.md (keeping only the README.md stub if it still serves a purpose, or deleting the directory entirely).

2. **In-repo single-file duplicates**: SD-021..023, TD-004..011, TP-002..020, ddx-concerns, helix.prd, helix.workflow.principles, product-vision, exec.FEAT-006.*, exec.FEAT-011.* — these IDs appear once in their canonical file but the docgraph scanner is finding them twice. Investigation needed: scanner may be picking up frontmatter blocks twice within the same file, or there's a scan-path overlap (e.g., .agents/skills/ or .claude/skills/ trees that contain doc copies). Run 'ddx doc audit -v' for verbose paths and 'rg -l "id: SD-021"' to find true duplicates.

Missing-dep (2): helix.workflow.principles → helix.workflow (the parent doc), and read-coverage-audit-2026-04-29 → plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture. Either restore the missing docs or update the depends_on.

.ddx/executions/20260502T034935-c7f9d929/prompt.md:3:    <title>Implement ADR-004: bead-backed collection abstraction with archive + attachments</title>
.ddx/executions/20260502T034935-c7f9d929/prompt.md:5:ADR-004 (Accepted) decided to generalize .ddx/beads.jsonl into a named-collection abstraction with separate archive and attachment storage, but the implementation hasn't shipped. Today the single beads.jsonl carries the entire history (5.4MB and growing) and the events array on closed beads is the dominant size driver. This epic implements the architecture per ADR-004.

Decisions to make during implementation (carry over from this conversation):
- Archival trigger: file-size threshold (e.g. >4MB), age-based (closed >30d), or count-based (>500 closed). ADR-004 doesn't pick one.
- Whether closed-bead events arrays move into attachment storage (.ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl) or stay inline in beads-archive.
- Migration semantics for the existing beads.jsonl (split in place vs explicit migrate command).

References: ADR-004, SD-004, FEAT-004, recent observation that beads.jsonl crossed the 5MB lefthook threshold during the 2026-05 redesign drain.

.ddx/executions/20260502T044444-ffba3487/prompt.md:3:    <title>Generalize bead store to named-collection abstraction (ADR-004 step 1)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260502T044444-ffba3487/prompt.md:5:Refactor cli/internal/bead/store.go (1687 lines, currently hard-codes .ddx/beads.jsonl) to accept a logical collection name. Introduce a CollectionRef abstraction so the same Store type can back 'beads' (active queue) and future collections like 'beads-archive', 'exec-runs', 'agent-sessions'. JSONL backend resolves a collection to a file under .ddx/<name>.jsonl. External (bd/br) backend keeps current behavior for the default 'beads' collection; non-default collections may stub out for this slice. No user-facing behavior change yet — default callers (ddx bead list/show/etc.) still operate on 'beads'.

References: ADR-004 §Decision items 1-3, store.go, backend.go, backend_external.go.
.ddx/executions/20260502T044812-68cf1712/prompt.md:3:    <title>Add beads-archive collection with transparent read-through (ADR-004 step 2)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260502T044812-68cf1712/prompt.md:5:Build on the named-collection abstraction to add a 'beads-archive' collection backed by .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl. Update bead reads (ddx bead list/show/ready/blocked, ddx work queue scan) so they transparently see archived beads when looked up by id, but treat the active 'beads' file as the queue source for ready/blocked computation. Archive remains append-only for normal commands; only the archive command (separate bead) writes there.

References: ADR-004 §Collection Model. Depends on ddx-2f453147.
.ddx/executions/20260502T045058-fd9e7a8c/prompt.md:3:    <title>Sidecar attachment storage for closed-bead events (ADR-004 step 3)</title>
.ddx/executions/20260502T045058-fd9e7a8c/prompt.md:5:Implement attachment sidecar storage at .ddx/attachments/<bead-id>/events.jsonl. The bead record in beads-archive carries a reference (preserved-extra field, e.g. 'attachments': {'events': '<relative-path>'}) instead of an inline events array, so the archive row stays small. ddx bead show lazy-loads events from the sidecar when present and merges them into the displayed history. Active beads keep events inline (no behavior change) — the move to sidecar only happens when a bead is archived (handled in the archive-command bead).

References: ADR-004 §Attachment Model. Depends on ddx-2f453147.

.ddx/executions/20260501T205031-39661a74/prompt.md:5:Operators should not need to author any project-level routing config (.ddx/config.yaml, --harness, --profile, --model, --provider) for `ddx work` to drain a project's bead queue. The platform should auto-select harness, model, and provider based on bead complexity, retry/fall back on failures, and decompose work that is too coarse for one dispatch.

## Observed behavior (HELIX project, 2026-05-01)

Running `ddx work --once --local` from `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`:

```
warning: triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts
▶ helix-13cfe23f: Align HELIX execution contract to shipped DDx execute-loop / execute-bead capabilities
✓ helix-13cfe23f → error: routing under-specified: pass at least one of Harness/Profile/ModelRef/Model/Provider, or auto-selection inputs
project: /Users/erik/Projects/helix
completed: 1  |  successes: 0  |  failures: 1
```

Workarounds tried, all failed or were rejected by users as the wrong layer to address this:

1. `ddx work --once --local --profile cheap` — same routing-under-specified error.
2. `ddx work --once --local --harness openrouter` — `harness "openrouter": no configured provider matches type "openrouter" (available: [])`, despite `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` having multiple openrouter-typed providers.
3. `--min-tier cheap` — unknown flag.

By contrast, the same `ddx work` invocation succeeds for the DDx project itself — workers in `ddx server workers list` show recent successful runs against ddx-* beads. So the per-project config gap is the difference, not anything global.

## Expected behavior

`ddx work` (and `ddx agent execute-loop` directly) should:

1. Auto-select routing for each bead with no flags and no project config required, given that the global agent config (`~/.config/agent/config.yaml`) has providers configured.
2. Make routing decisions from bead complexity: doc-only or single-file changes → cheap tier; multi-file refactors → standard; cross-cutting work → smart. The 'triage complexity gate' warning suggests this exists but is disabled by default.
3. Retry on transient failure within the same tier; escalate to a higher tier on substantive failure (this is documented behavior — `winning_tier=exhausted` events show the escalation chain works once dispatched, but auto-routing has to dispatch first).
4. When a bead is too coarse for any tier to execute reliably, either decompose it into child beads or surface a 'needs operator triage' status with a clear reason — never silently wedge with `routing under-specified`.
5. Default to using the global agent config's `default_provider` when no project override exists, instead of treating the absence of project config as a hard error.

## Why this matters

- **Adoption friction**: Every new project that adopts DDx hits this wall. Authoring `.ddx/config.yaml` requires understanding DDx's routing internals before an operator can run `ddx work` once.
- **Methodology coupling**: HELIX's value proposition includes 'supervised autopilot' — the autopilot is supposed to drain ready beads. When `ddx work` requires per-project routing config, HELIX projects have to ship operational setup that distracts from the methodology.
- **Operator surprise**: The DDx-vs-HELIX boundary contract (HELIX/CONTRACT-001) explicitly says HELIX should not own routing config. But projects can't run `ddx work` without it. That's a contract violation manifesting as setup burden.
- **Triage gate disabled by default**: The warning 'triage complexity gate is disabled; coarse beads may waste dispatch attempts' is emitted on every run but the operator has no obvious way to enable it. The default should be enabled with a documented opt-out, not the inverse.

## Suggested approach (not prescriptive)

1. Audit the auto-selection input requirements: what inputs are needed to pick a tier when no project config is present? Likely the bead's labels, `kind`, file-touch estimate, and prior-attempt history. All available without operator input.
2. Make 'use global agent config defaults' the fallback when project routing is unspecified — currently the code requires explicit project routing OR explicit flags.
3. Enable triage complexity gate by default; document the opt-out flag.
4. Add an integration test: `ddx work --once --local` succeeds against a fresh project with no `.ddx/config.yaml` and a single bead in `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, given a minimal `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` with one provider.

## Out of scope

- Changing the global agent config schema. The fix is to reduce dependency on per-project config, not redesign the global one.
- Per-bead model overrides for advanced users — those should remain available via flags but should not be required for the common case.

## Reference

- HELIX alignment review where this surfaced: `docs/helix/06-iterate/alignment-reviews/AR-2026-05-01-public-examples.md` (in the easel/helix repo). The audit's 12 follow-up beads were drained via direct sub-agent dispatch instead of `ddx work` because of this gap.

.ddx/executions/20260503T030914-52663e20/prompt.md:5:Today execute-bead's no_changes outcome conflates two cases:
1. Legitimate already_satisfied: the AC is currently met by prior commits; agent correctly says 'nothing to do'
2. Silent failure: agent didn't know how to do the work, gave up, wrote no_changes_rationale.txt; loop closes the bead anyway

Both result in the same artifact (no_changes_rationale.txt) and the same close. The second case is a serious failure mode masquerading as success.

**Constraint (per ADR-004)**: bd/br compatibility forbids adding new persisted status values. The bead's status stays in the canonical 6 (open/in_progress/closed/blocked/proposed/cancelled). DDx-specific execution semantics live in LABELS or EVENTS, NOT in new statuses.

Fix has two halves:

A) Execute-bead PROMPT change (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go executeBeadInstructionsClaudeText / AgentText):
- When the agent decides 'no changes', it MUST include in the no_changes_rationale.txt one of:
  - verification_command: <shell command that proves the AC is currently met> AND its output showing pass
  - status: needs_investigation + reason: <why I cannot make progress>
- Bare 'no changes' without either is rejected by the loop

B) LOOP-side verification (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go):
- Parse no_changes_rationale.txt
- If verification_command present: run it; if exit 0, legitimate already_satisfied → close. If non-zero, fail to verify → bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:no-changes-unverified' added + emit no_changes_unverified event
- If status: needs_investigation: bead STAYS OPEN with label 'triage:needs-investigation' added (NOT a status change), event captures the agent's reason
- If neither present: reject — bead stays open with label 'triage:no-changes-unjustified' added + emit no_changes_unjustified event

Status NEVER changes to needs_investigation (that would violate bd/br compatibility). Auto-triage (ddx-3c154349) consumes the labels + events to drive its triage policy.
.ddx/executions/20260503T025809-45a220f7/prompt.md:5:The bug at ddx-29058e2a (5 CLI flags silently dropped on the server path) is a symptom of a deeper architectural issue: the ExecuteLoopSpec is defined as parallel structs that must be hand-maintained in lockstep. Adding any flag requires editing 5+ places, and forgetting one is silent.

CURRENT STATE (the smell)

Each flag traverses 6 boundaries:

1. cobra extract: cmd.Flags().GetX("flag") → local var (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1573-1611)
2. positional param: passed to executeLoopWithServer / runExecuteLoopLocal (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1627, ~1941 signature)
3. loose JSON map: workerSpec map[string]interface{} (cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:~1948)
4. server request struct: hand-maintained in handleStartExecuteLoopWorker (cli/internal/server/server.go:2246-2260)
5. ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec literal: server.go:2289-2303 → cli/internal/server/workers.go:25-49
6. runWorker per-field consumption: cli/internal/server/workers.go ~656

ALSO IN SCOPE — the GraphQL parallel path (per codex review):
- workerDispatchAdapter at cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go:62-75, 123-137 has its own request struct AND its own StartExecuteLoop literal — same parallel-struct smell on a different transport. Unification must DELETE this too, otherwise the refactor fixes REST and leaves GraphQL re-broken.

Two of those (steps 4 and 5) are typed but maintained in parallel. The 5 dropped flags (opaque_passthrough, max_cost, request_timeout, min_power, max_power) fell out exactly there. PLUS FromRev is also dropped on the server path (cobra flag at agent_cmd.go:1560 + work.go:58, consumed locally at agent_cmd.go:1753 ExecuteBeadRuntime{FromRev: fromRev}, never reaches the server). The bug fix would be local — add fields in 5 places — but the ROOT CAUSE recurs the next time a flag is added.

DESIGN — single canonical ExecuteLoopSpec struct

Define one struct, used at every layer:

  cobra RunE → parseExecuteLoopSpec(cmd) (ExecuteLoopSpec, DispatchOptions)
                ↓
       (--local) runExecuteLoopInline(spec)
                ↓
       (default) httpSubmitExecuteLoop(spec)  ── JSON ──▶ server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker
                                                   ↓ (decode INTO THE SAME struct, then Validate + ApplyDefaults)
                                                startExecuteLoop(spec)
                                                   ↓ (persist as spec.json — SAME struct, fully-resolved)
                                                runWorker(spec)

Properties:
- One struct definition. JSON tags drive both wire format and disk persistence.
- Cobra binding is the only per-flag code (irreducible — cobra needs to know each flag).
- --local and server paths consume the same struct; behavior parity is structural, not asserted by tests.
- Adding a flag = (a) one struct field + (b) one cobra binding. Drop is impossible by construction — the JSON tag round-trips through wire and persistence.
- DispatchOptions is a SEPARATE small struct holding control-plane flags (--local, --json) that are NOT execution-spec fields and must NOT be persisted on disk.

CRITICAL CONTRACTS (per codex review)

1. Wire format for time.Duration: Go's default time.Duration JSON marshaling produces nanoseconds, but current code sends poll_interval as a string (agent_cmd.go:1969-1971). The unified spec MUST commit to one format — either custom Duration type with string MarshalJSON, or canonical numeric. Pick string (operator-readable spec.json wins) and add UnmarshalJSON tolerating both.

2. Validate + ApplyDefaults lifecycle:
   - parseExecuteLoopSpec returns spec + DispatchOptions, with cobra defaults applied
   - Spec.Validate() is called BEFORE HTTP submit (client-side guard)
   - server.handleStartExecuteLoopWorker decodes → calls Spec.ApplyDefaults() → Spec.Validate() BEFORE StartExecuteLoop (server cannot trust the wire)
   - GraphQL workerDispatchAdapter goes through THE SAME StartExecuteLoop entry point so Validate + ApplyDefaults are guaranteed
   - spec.json on disk is fully-resolved (no implicit defaults at runWorker time)

3. Forward-compat stance: ddx is single-repo, single-version client/server today. Add SpecVersion int field; server returns 400 with capability list if SpecVersion > supported. Newer-server-older-client: existing fields unchanged, new fields ignored on both sides (the current default). Make this explicit in the AC; do not rely on json's silent unknown-field tolerance as the only contract.

4. Backward-compat for old persisted specs: existing spec.json files in .ddx/workers/ have legacy fields (e.g., min_tier/max_tier in older specs at .ddx/workers/worker-20260422T083934-f77c/spec.json:5-6). UnmarshalJSON must tolerate unknown fields when LOADING from disk (json.Decoder default behavior is fine here; do NOT enable DisallowUnknownFields for disk reads).

LOCATION

Likely cli/internal/agent/spec.go or cli/internal/agent/executeloop/spec.go (a small focused package). Importable from both cli/cmd/ and cli/internal/server/ without import cycles. NOTE: REACH-PROTO (ddx-a946c744) needs to know about this new package as an entry-root for the production-reachability checker.

NOT IN SCOPE

- Other commands (ddx agent run, ddx try, ddx run, MCP agent dispatch, ddx work passthrough variants) — same smell may exist; covered by the sister audit bead ddx-fb290074.
- Reworking client→server transport (HTTP/JSON stays as-is).
- Changing validation logic itself — only ensuring it lives on the spec so both paths apply it.
- Renaming flags or reshaping CLI surface.
- Reshaping LabelFilter from string to []string — that is a picker semantics change, owned by ddx-9d55601f. Keep LabelFilter as string to keep ddx-9d55601f landing in parallel.

USER-OBSERVED SYMPTOM (originally what filed this bead)
- ddx work --harness claude works under --local but fails when going through the server.
- Root cause: opaque_passthrough was dropped at the server's request struct. With OpaquePassthrough lost, the server-spawned worker validates the harness against its provider list (which rejects 'claude') instead of treating it as opaque-passthrough.
- The unification refactor solves this by construction — there's no second struct to omit OpaquePassthrough from. NOTE: OpaquePassthrough has TWO skip points to verify (cli/internal/server/workers.go:314-317 AND :752-773), not one.

ABSORBS THE NARROW FIX
The 5 originally-listed dropped flags + FromRev all become fields on the unified spec. They cannot be dropped after this refactor. The refactor IS the fix.

SEQUENCING WITH IN-FLIGHT BEADS

- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): touches execute_bead_loop.go's Run loop. Should land BEFORE this OR be tightly coordinated. If unification lands first, dc157075 redoes defaulting + persistence. Recommend dc157075 first.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker priority bug): does NOT block on this. Keep LabelFilter type stable. Picker fix lands in parallel.
- ddx-a946c744 (REACH-PROTO): needs to know about new cli/internal/agent/executeloop/ package as entry root.

Category: correctness
Severity: critical
Description: executeOnService currently does not carry DDx-owned execution intent correctly. With the updated agent interface, DDx must pass MinPower/MaxPower and opaque passthrough constraints to Execute, then record the agent's actual model/power response. Without that, DDx cannot own bead retry escalation or audit operator constraints.
Suggested fix: Populate ServiceExecuteRequest.MinPower/MaxPower and Harness/Provider/Model from the narrow passthrough envelope, capture actual model/power from Execute response/events, and persist requested_min_power/requested_max_power, actual model/power, plus requested passthrough in run and attempt evidence.
